title: Seborrheic dermatitis
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Seborrheic dermatitis is a common inflammatory skin condition with flaky white or yellowish scale on oily areas such as the scalp, face, ears, or chest.
Symptoms can include scale, dandruff, itching, mild redness, and greasy or oily skin areas.
Over-the-counter dandruff or medicated shampoos may help mild flaking and dryness.
Patients should contact a clinician if symptoms do not respond to self-care or if patches drain fluid or pus, crust, become very red, or become painful.
